rfc_references.title: ZLIB Compressed Data Format Specification version 3.3
DEFLATE Compressed Data Format Specification version 1.3

derived_from: RFC 1950 — ZLIB Compressed Data Format Specification (May 1996)
RFC 1951 — DEFLATE Compressed Data Format Specification (May 1996)
https://zlib.net/zlib.h — public header file (API declarations only, not implementation)
not_derived_from: Any other zlib C implementation source file
provenance.notes: This spec constitutes the clean-room boundary. The implementation team reads this spec only. The spec team read the public documentation listed above.

return_codes.name: Z_STREAM_END
return_codes.description: End of compressed data stream reached; all input consumed and output produced

return_codes.name: Z_NEED_DICT
return_codes.description: Preset dictionary required; call inflateSetDictionary before continuing

return_codes.name: Z_ERRNO
return_codes.description: System I/O error; check errno for detail

return_codes.name: Z_STREAM_ERROR
return_codes.description: Inconsistent stream state or invalid parameter (e.g. null strm, bad level)

return_codes.name: Z_DATA_ERROR
return_codes.description: Invalid or corrupt input data; checksum mismatch or invalid block type

return_codes.name: Z_BUF_ERROR
return_codes.description: Output buffer full (avail_out == 0) or no progress possible (avail_in and avail_out both 0)

return_codes.name: Z_VERSION_ERROR
return_codes.description: zlib library version incompatible with header version used at compile time

compression_levels.name: Z_NO_COMPRESSION
compression_levels.description: Store only — no compression applied; output is still a valid zlib stream

compression_levels.name: Z_BEST_SPEED
compression_levels.description: Fastest compression; minimum CPU, maximum output size

compression_levels.name: Z_BEST_COMPRESSION
compression_levels.description: Best compression ratio; maximum CPU, minimum output size

compression_levels.name: Z_DEFAULT_COMPRESSION
compression_levels.description: Default compression level; equivalent to level 6 in current implementations

flush_modes.name: Z_NO_FLUSH
flush_modes.description: Normal operation; accumulate data and output as convenient

flush_modes.name: Z_PARTIAL_FLUSH
flush_modes.description: Flush enough output to decompress to the next sync point (deprecated; use Z_SYNC_FLUSH)

flush_modes.name: Z_SYNC_FLUSH
flush_modes.description: Flush all pending output to the output buffer, aligned to a byte boundary

flush_modes.name: Z_FULL_FLUSH
flush_modes.description: Flush and reset compression state; allows recovery from corrupt data mid-stream

flush_modes.name: Z_FINISH
flush_modes.description: Finish and close the stream; all remaining input is consumed and output produced

flush_modes.name: Z_BLOCK
flush_modes.description: Stop at next DEFLATE block boundary

compression_strategies.name: Z_FILTERED
compression_strategies.description: Data produced by a filter (small values with random distribution); Huffman-heavy

compression_strategies.name: Z_HUFFMAN_ONLY
compression_strategies.description: Force Huffman encoding only; no string matching

compression_strategies.name: Z_RLE
compression_strategies.description: Limit match distances to one; nearly as fast as Z_HUFFMAN_ONLY for PNG

compression_strategies.name: Z_FIXED
compression_strategies.description: Use fixed Huffman codes; produces reproducible output

data_types.name: Z_TEXT
data_types.description: Data appears to be text (Z_ASCII alias exists)

window_bits.name: WBITS_MIN
window_bits.description: Minimum window bits for deflate (256-byte window)

window_bits.name: WBITS_MAX
window_bits.description: Maximum window bits for deflate (32768-byte window)

window_bits.name: WBITS_GZIP_OFFSET
window_bits.description: Add to windowBits to produce gzip-format output (e.g. 15+16=31)

window_bits.name: WBITS_RAW_NEGATE
window_bits.description: Negate windowBits for raw deflate output (no zlib or gzip header)

z_stream.description: Compression/decompression state. Must be zero-initialized before use. The zalloc, zfree, and opaque fields may be set before deflateInit/inflateInit; if left NULL the library uses its own allocator.

fields.name: next_in
fields.description: Pointer to next available input byte

fields.name: avail_in
fields.description: Number of bytes remaining at next_in

fields.name: total_in
fields.description: Total bytes consumed from input so far

fields.name: next_out
fields.description: Pointer to next output position

fields.name: avail_out
fields.description: Remaining space at next_out

fields.name: total_out
fields.description: Total bytes written to output so far

fields.name: msg
fields.description: Last error message string, or NULL

fields.name: state
fields.description: Internal opaque state; must not be modified by application

fields.name: zalloc
fields.description: Application-supplied allocation function, or NULL for default

fields.name: zfree
fields.description: Application-supplied deallocation function, or NULL for default

fields.name: opaque
fields.description: Passed to zalloc/zfree as first argument

fields.name: data_type
fields.description: Library's best guess at input data type (Z_BINARY, Z_TEXT, Z_UNKNOWN)

fields.name: adler
fields.description: Running Adler-32 checksum of uncompressed data (or CRC-32 in gzip mode)

fields.name: reserved
fields.description: Reserved; must be zero

z_stream.notes: Pass ZLIB_VERSION string and sizeof(z_stream) to deflateInit_/inflateInit_ (the underlying functions behind the deflateInit/inflateInit macros) so the library can detect version and struct-size mismatches.

compress.description: Single-call compression using default level. Equivalent to compress2 with level=Z_DEFAULT_COMPRESSION.

parameters.name: destLen
parameters.description: On entry: capacity of dest. On exit: actual compressed size.

postconditions: On Z_OK, *destLen holds the actual compressed byte count
Output can be decompressed by uncompress() to recover the original bytes

compress2.description: Single-call compression with caller-specified compression level.

parameters.name: destLen
parameters.description: On entry: capacity. On exit: actual compressed size.

return_values.Z_STREAM_ERROR: level is not Z_DEFAULT_COMPRESSION and not in [0,9]

preconditions: level must be Z_DEFAULT_COMPRESSION (-1) or an integer in the range [0, 9]

uncompress.description: Single-call decompression. Caller must supply an output buffer large enough for the uncompressed result.

parameters.name: destLen
parameters.description: On entry: capacity of dest. On exit: actual uncompressed size.

return_values.Z_BUF_ERROR: Output buffer too small or input was empty
return_values.Z_DATA_ERROR: Input data is corrupt or was not compressed with zlib

compressBound.description: Returns an upper bound on the output buffer size required for compress() or compress2() with the given source length. Always call this to size the destination buffer rather than guessing.

return_values.positive_value: Upper bound in bytes; guaranteed >= actual compressed output size for any compression level

postconditions: Return value >= actual compressed size for any valid sourceLen and any compression level

crc32.description: Incremental CRC-32 computation. To start a new checksum, pass crc=0 and buf=non-NULL. To obtain the initial value, pass buf=NULL. Used in gzip format (RFC 1952).

parameters.name: crc
parameters.description: Running CRC-32 value; use 0 to start a new checksum

parameters.name: buf
parameters.description: Input bytes, or NULL to retrieve the initial CRC value (0)

postconditions: crc32(crc32(0, A, lenA), B, lenB) == crc32(0, concat(A,B), lenA+lenB)

adler32.description: Incremental Adler-32 computation. Initial value is 1 (not 0). Used in zlib format (RFC 1950).

parameters.name: adler
parameters.description: Running Adler-32; use 1 to start a new checksum

parameters.name: buf
parameters.description: Input bytes, or NULL to retrieve initial value (1)

postconditions: adler32(adler32(1, A, lenA), B, lenB) == adler32(1, concat(A,B), lenA+lenB)

zlibVersion.description: Returns the version string of the zlib library currently loaded at runtime. Used internally by deflateInit_/inflateInit_ for version compatibility checking.

return_values.non_null: Null-terminated ASCII version string, e.g. '1.2.11' or '1.3'

postconditions: Return value starts with '1.' for all 1.x releases

zError.description: Maps a zlib return code to a human-readable error string.

parameters.name: err
parameters.description: A zlib return code (Z_OK, Z_DATA_ERROR, etc.)

invariants.description: Z_OK must equal 0
Z_STREAM_END must equal 1
Z_NEED_DICT must equal 2
Z_ERRNO must equal -1
Z_STREAM_ERROR must equal -2
Z_DATA_ERROR must equal -3
Z_MEM_ERROR must equal -4
Z_BUF_ERROR must equal -5
Z_VERSION_ERROR must equal -6
zlibVersion() must return a non-NULL string starting with '1.' for all 1.x releases
crc32(0, NULL, 0) must return 0 — the initial CRC-32 value (RFC 1952 Section 8)
crc32(0, 'hello', 5) must equal 0x3610a686 (907060870) — standard test vector
adler32(1, NULL, 0) must return 1 — the initial Adler-32 value (RFC 1950 Section 9)
Incremental CRC-32 over two chunks must equal one-shot CRC-32 of the concatenation
Incremental Adler-32 over two chunks must equal one-shot Adler-32 of the concatenation
compress2() returns Z_OK for valid input with sufficient output buffer
compress2() returns Z_STREAM_ERROR when level=10 (invalid; valid range is -1 or 0-9)
compress() then uncompress() must recover the original bytes exactly for any input
First 2 bytes of zlib output: (CMF * 256 + FLG) % 31 == 0 — RFC 1950 Section 2.2 header checksum
CM field (lower 4 bits of first byte) must be 8, indicating DEFLATE compression method — RFC 1950 Section 2.2
Last 4 bytes of zlib output are the Adler-32 checksum of the uncompressed data in big-endian — RFC 1950 Section 2.2

assertions.description: Last 4 bytes == Adler-32 of original input in big-endian

invariants.description: uncompress() must return Z_DATA_ERROR when source is not valid zlib-compressed data
compressBound(n) must return a value >= the actual compressed output size for any n-byte input at any compression level

zlib_rfc1950.description: zlib compressed data format. All multi-byte integers are big-endian.

fields.name: CMF
fields.description: Compression Method and Flags byte

fields.name: CM
fields.description: Compression method. Must be 8 (DEFLATE). Values 0-7 and 9-15 are not defined.

fields.name: CINFO
fields.description: Window size: 2^(CINFO+8). For CM=8, CINFO must not exceed 7 (32768-byte window). Value 8 and above are undefined.

fields.name: FLG
fields.description: Flags byte

fields.name: FCHECK
fields.description: Check bits. Set so that CMF*256+FLG is a multiple of 31.

fields.name: FDICT
fields.description: Preset dictionary flag. If 1, a DICTID follows the FLG byte. compress/compress2 always produce FDICT=0.

fields.name: FLEVEL
fields.description: Compression level indicator. 0=fastest, 1=fast, 2=default, 3=maximum. Informational only; does not affect decompression.

fields.name: COMPRESSED_DATA
fields.description: DEFLATE compressed data stream (RFC 1951)

fields.name: ADLER32
fields.constraint: equals adler32 of the original uncompressed data, big-endian
fields.description: Adler-32 checksum of the uncompressed data, stored big-endian. Covers all uncompressed bytes.

gzip_rfc1952.description: gzip file format. Fixed 10-byte header, compressed data, then CRC-32 and ISIZE trailer.

fields.name: ID1
fields.description: Magic byte 1 (0x1f)

fields.name: ID2
fields.description: Magic byte 2 (0x8b)

fields.name: CM
fields.description: Compression method; must be 8 (DEFLATE)

fields.name: FLG
fields.description: Flags (FTEXT, FHCRC, FEXTRA, FNAME, FCOMMENT)

fields.name: MTIME
fields.description: Modification time as Unix epoch seconds, little-endian. 0 if not available.

fields.name: XFL
fields.description: Extra flags: 2=maximum compression, 4=fastest algorithm. 0 otherwise.

fields.name: OS
fields.description: Operating system. 0=FAT, 3=Unix, 7=Macintosh, 11=NTFS, 255=unknown.

fields.name: CRC32
fields.constraint: equals crc32 of all uncompressed bytes, little-endian
fields.description: CRC-32 of the uncompressed data, little-endian

fields.name: ISIZE
fields.description: Size of the original uncompressed input modulo 2^32, little-endian

deflate_rfc1951.description: Raw DEFLATE compressed data. No header or checksum — just the compressed bit stream. Used as the payload inside both zlib (RFC 1950) and gzip (RFC 1952) formats.

fields.name: BFINAL
fields.description: Set to 1 in the last block of the compressed data stream, 0 otherwise

fields.name: BTYPE
fields.description: Block compression type: 00=no compression, 01=fixed Huffman, 10=dynamic Huffman, 11=reserved (error)

fields.name: BLOCK_DATA
fields.description: Block data; format depends on BTYPE. For BTYPE=00: 4-byte LEN/NLEN header then literal bytes. For BTYPE=01/10: Huffman-coded (length, distance) pairs and literals.

error_model.return_code_semantics: All zlib functions that can fail return a signed integer. Non-negative values are success or informational (Z_OK=0, Z_STREAM_END=1, Z_NEED_DICT=2). Negative values are errors. The convention is consistent across all API functions.
error_model.error_stickiness: After Z_DATA_ERROR or Z_STREAM_ERROR, the stream is invalid. The application must call deflateEnd or inflateEnd. Continuing to use the stream after an error produces undefined results. Z_BUF_ERROR is not fatal — it means no progress was made in the current call and the application should adjust avail_in or avail_out and retry.

error_codes.name: Z_STREAM_END
error_codes.meaning: All input has been consumed and all output has been produced (only when flush=Z_FINISH)

error_codes.name: Z_NEED_DICT
error_codes.meaning: A preset dictionary is needed; call inflateSetDictionary with the same dictionary used during compression

error_codes.name: Z_ERRNO
error_codes.meaning: A system-level I/O error occurred; check errno

error_codes.name: Z_STREAM_ERROR
error_codes.meaning: The stream structure was inconsistent (e.g. next_in or next_out was NULL) or an invalid parameter was passed

error_codes.name: Z_DATA_ERROR
error_codes.meaning: The input data was invalid or corrupt (bad block type, invalid code lengths, checksum mismatch)

error_codes.name: Z_MEM_ERROR
error_codes.meaning: There was not enough memory to complete the operation

error_codes.name: Z_BUF_ERROR
error_codes.meaning: No progress was possible (not fatal): avail_in=0 and avail_out=0, or output buffer was full. Adjust and retry.

error_codes.name: Z_VERSION_ERROR
error_codes.meaning: The version of the zlib library linked at runtime does not match the version of zlib.h used to compile the application
